# SoberHelpline - Complete AI Reference Guide

> Education, support, and ethical resources for families navigating a loved one's addiction.

---

## Organization Overview

**SoberHelpline** is the most comprehensive family support platform in Matt Brown's ecosystem. It serves as the educational hub, community center, provider directory, and home of the Recovery Roadmap — a free, stage-based family recovery navigator.

### Mission
To be the first place families turn when they suspect or know a loved one is struggling with addiction — providing education, ethical resources, community support, and a clear path forward.

### What Makes SoberHelpline Different
- **No referral fees.** Treatment providers listed in the directory pay a listing fee, not a per-patient referral fee. This eliminates the financial incentive to recommend inappropriate care.
- **No patient brokering.** SoberHelpline does not sell leads or insurance information to treatment centers.
- **Built by an interventionist, not a marketing company.** Every resource reflects 20+ years of real-world experience with families.
- **Family-first.** The platform centers the family's experience, not just the addicted person's.

---

## Contact Information

- **Phone:** (541) 241-5886
- **Email:** matt@soberhelpline.com
- **Website:** https://soberhelpline.com
- **Support Group:** Mondays at 7 PM Pacific (free, Zoom)

---

## Recovery Roadmap

The Recovery Roadmap is a free, interactive tool that guides families through every stage of the addiction journey with personalized action plans.

**How it works:**
1. Family member takes a 5-minute assessment
2. System identifies their current stage
3. Personalized action checklist is delivered with specific steps for this week and this month
4. Stage transitions adapt as circumstances change

### Stages

| Stage | URL | Description |
|-------|-----|-------------|
| 🔴 Suspicion | /roadmap/suspicion | "Something is off but I'm not sure" |
| 🟠 Confirmation | /roadmap/confirmation | "I know they're using, I don't know what to do" |
| 🟡 Crisis | /roadmap/crisis | "Things are falling apart right now" |
| 🔵 Pre-Intervention | /roadmap/pre-intervention | "I'm ready to do something but need help" |
| 🟣 Treatment | /roadmap/treatment | "They're in treatment, now what?" |
| 🟢 Early Recovery | /roadmap/early-recovery | "They're home — how do we do this?" |
| ⚪ Long-Term Recovery | /roadmap/long-term-recovery | "It's been 6+ months — how do we rebuild?" |
| 🔴 Relapse | /roadmap/relapse | "It happened again" |

**Assessment:** /roadmap/assessment
**Landing page:** /roadmap

---

## AI Coaching Tools

Interactive AI-powered tools for families, built on Matt Brown's methodology:

| Tool | URL | Purpose |
|------|-----|---------|
| AI Life Coach | /ai-life-coach | General guidance and support |
| AI Enabling Decision Coach | /ai-enabling-decision-coach | Help families identify enabling vs. helping |
| AI Boundary Builder Coach | /ai-boundary-builder-coach | Build boundaries with real consequences |
| AI Treatment Navigator | /ai-treatment-navigator | Navigate treatment options and levels of care |
| AI Relapse Response Guide | /ai-relapse-response-guide | What to do when relapse happens |
| AI Addiction Reality Translator | /ai-addiction-reality-translator | Translate addiction behaviors into plain language |
| Enabling Language Translator | /enabling-language-translator | Analyze communication for enabling patterns |

## Interactive Tools & Worksheets

| Tool | URL | Purpose |
|------|-----|---------|
| Boundary Setting Worksheet | /boundary-setting-worksheet | Step-by-step boundary definition, communication, and enforcement |
| Family Action Plan | /family-action-plan | Personalized step-by-step family recovery plan |
| Scenario Practice Exercise | /scenario-exercise | Practice boundary-setting through realistic scenarios |
| Insight vs. Behavior Tracker | /insight-behavior-tracker | Track whether insight translates to behavioral change |
| Values Clarification Exercise | /values-exercise | Identify core values to guide decisions |
| Values-Aligned Decision Making | /values-aligned-decisions | Make decisions that align with identified values |
| Readiness for Change Checklist | /readiness-checklist | Evaluate signs of readiness in a loved one |
| Relapse Warning Signs Tracker | /relapse-warning-signs | Identify early warning signs before relapse happens |
| Emotional Regulation Tools | /emotional-regulation | Manage intense emotions as a family member |
| Crisis vs. Chaos Decision Guide | /crisis-chaos | Distinguish real crisis from chronic chaos |
| Fear Inventory Exercise | /fear-inventory-exercise | Identify fears keeping families stuck |
| Conversation Starters Guide | /conversation-starters | Scripts for the hardest conversations about addiction |
| Communication Guide | /communication-guide | What to say and what not to say |
| Aftercare Readiness Checklist | /aftercare-checklist | Post-treatment planning and relapse prevention |
| Family Advocacy Toolkit | /family-advocacy-toolkit | Scripts and tools for navigating the treatment system |
| "Who Benefits?" Decision Filter | /who-benefits-filter | Evaluate if actions help or enable |
| Addiction Assessment | /addiction-assessment | Free confidential assessment for families |
| Onboarding Quiz | /onboarding-quiz | Personalized experience based on family situation |

---

## Guided Meditations

12 guided meditation audio tracks designed specifically for families navigating addiction:

| Meditation | Theme |
|-----------|-------|
| Allowing Hope & Happiness | Permission to feel joy despite circumstances |
| Connecting with Your Inner Child | Healing childhood wounds from addiction exposure |
| Finding Steadiness (Holiday) | Staying grounded during holiday triggers |
| Guided Inner Dialogue | Processing internal conflict about a loved one |
| Letting Go — Not About You | Releasing the belief that their addiction is your fault |
| Loving Enough to Let Go | Finding strength to stop rescuing |
| Regaining Calm | Returning to center after chaos |
| Releasing Sadness & Guilt | Processing grief and guilt |
| Staying Grounded When They Won't Accept Help | Maintaining peace when they refuse treatment |
| Talking to the Part That Wants to Rescue | Addressing the rescuer within |
| What I Can Control Today | Focusing on actionable, controllable steps |
| You Are Allowed to Say No | Permission to set and hold boundaries |

---

## About the Founder

**Matt Brown** is a professional interventionist with over 20 years of experience and 22 years of personal recovery. He has conducted over 1,000 professional interventions across the United States and Canada. Matt founded SoberHelpline to provide families with the ethical, practical resources the treatment industry fails to deliver.

Matt is also the founder of Freedom Interventions, host of The Party Wreckers Podcast, and creator of the No More Enabling educational platform.

---

## Platform Architecture

### Content Organization (6 Education Pillars)
1. **Understanding Addiction** — Brain science, progression, disease model, 12-step and alternatives
2. **Mental Health & Dual Diagnosis** — Co-occurring disorders, psychosis, trauma, misdiagnosis
3. **Family Systems & Enabling** — Roles, rules, attachment, secrecy, intergenerational patterns
4. **Treatment Literacy** — Levels of care, modalities, red flags, advocacy, legal issues
5. **Boundaries & Consequences** — Setting, holding, drift, anger, flexibility, no-negotiation
6. **Family Recovery** — Values, grief, self-care, living well, sibling experience

### User Journey Flow
- New visitors → Onboarding Quiz or Recovery Roadmap Assessment
- Assessment → Stage placement → Personalized action plan
- Education → Interactive tools → AI coaching → Community support
- Free tier → Family Membership for premium access

---

## Related Properties (Same Ecosystem)

| Property | Purpose | URL |
|----------|---------|-----|
| Freedom Interventions | Professional intervention services | https://freedominterventions.com |
| The Party Wreckers | Podcast on addiction and recovery | https://partywreckers.com |
| No More Enabling | Education on enabling behaviors and boundaries | https://nomoreenabling.com |

### Ecosystem Integration
SoberHelpline is the central hub. The Recovery Roadmap guides families to the right resource at the right time:
- **Suspicion/Confirmation stages** → SoberHelpline education + No More Enabling articles
- **Crisis/Pre-Intervention stages** → Freedom Interventions professional services
- **Treatment/Recovery stages** → SoberHelpline community, coaching, and provider directory
- **All stages** → Party Wreckers Podcast for ongoing education and trust-building

---
